revision.text:
[[ja:百度参り]] {{Multiple issues|sources=August 24, 2017|original research=August 24, 2017}} [[File:Hyakudoishi Stone and stele for Maruhashi Shizuko's poem in Yasaka Shrine in Kokura Castle.JPG|thumb|right|A Hyakudoishi with counting beads
({{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)|ja|八坂神社 (北九州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Kitakyushu)|qq=}}・Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|qid=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}})]] '''Hyakudo Mairi''' (百度参り) refers to the Japanese folk practice of visiting the same shrine or temple 100 times to make a wish or prayer. It is also called '''Ohyakudo''' (お百度). == Overview == The wishes made during Hyakudo Mairi are often personal and deeply significant, reflecting a belief that repeated visits increase the likelihood of fulfillment. Originally, the practice involved visiting nearby {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} shrines or well-known religious sites daily for 100 days, known as ''Hyakunichi Mōde'' (百日詣). Over time, it evolved into a more condensed form, performed 100 times in a single day, to accommodate urgent prayers. References to Hyakudo Mairi appear as early as the {{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|qid=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}, a historical chronicle. For example, on August 10, 1189 (文治5), it records women performing Hyakudo Mairi at {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qid=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} to pray for success in the conquest of the Northern provinces. Similarly, in 1241 (仁治2), {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō clan|1=Hōjō clan}} members made a hundred prayers at the same shrine. == Method == During Hyakudo Mairi, participants move between the shrine or temple entrance and the main hall (or worship area) 100 times, a practice commonly referred to as "stepping the Ohyakudo" (お百度を踏む). Many shrines and temples feature a {{ill|Hyakudoishi|ja|百度石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakudoishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百度参り|1=Hyakudo Mairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580748}}, a milestone or marker near the entrance used to help track the number of visits. Worshippers may use small stones, paper strips, or bamboo sticks—placing one at the altar each time—to avoid miscounting. Some Hyakudoishi are equipped with abacus-like devices for this purpose. It is said that conducting the ritual barefoot or in secrecy enhances its effectiveness. File:Shitennoji16s3200.jpg|Temple Hyakudoishi ({{ill|Shitennō-ji|en|Shitennō-ji|qid=Q339859|lt=Shitennō-ji|1=Shitennō-ji}}, Osaka) File:Hyakudoishi.jpeg|Shrine Hyakudoishi ({{ill|Katano Shrine|ja|片埜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katano Shrine|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture) File:Kompira Hyakudoishi.jpeg|Hyakudoishi with an abacus ({{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}}, Kagawa Prefecture) File:Tamura-jinja%28Takamatsu%29_hyakudoishi.jpeg|Large Hyakudoishi with a bead counter ({{ill|Tamura Shrine (Takamatsu)|ja|田村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Shrine (Takamatsu)|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture) == See Also == * {{ill|Nana-toko Mairi|ja|ななとこまいり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nana-toko Mairi|1=Q11274400|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11274400}} * {{ill|Ishikiri Shrine|ja|石切剣箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Shrine|1=Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11585189}} (Higashiosaka) – Famous for Hyakudo Mairi, where worshippers can often be seen performing the ritual. * {{ill|Nyoi-ji (Gojō)|ja|如意寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoi-ji (Gojō)|1=Nyoi-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56349697}} – The origin of its Hyakudo Mairi lies in a 100-day prayer for a woman with uterine cancer. * {{ill|Gosho Sennodo Mairi|ja|御所千度参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Sennodo Mairi|1=Gosho Sendo Mairi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489054}} – A mass gathering for thousandfold prayers in Kyoto during the late Edo period. This is a variation of Hyakudo Mairi. * The television drama ''{{ill|Hakusen Nagashi|ja|白線流し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusen Nagashi|1=Hakusen Nagashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5356912}}'' features a scene where characters perform Hyakudo Mairi to pray for a friend’s recovery. {{Commonscat|Hyakudoishi|Hyakudoishi}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|百度参り|version=104589610|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:百度参り]] {{wikidata link|Q11580748}} [[Category:Japanese folk religion]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wish Offerings]]
